methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Skyryse, a Series C aerospace company, is valued at $1.15B post-money following $500M+ in cumulative capital raised. The valuation is benchmarked against a comp universe of 5 public Space & Aerospace names (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, including Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, and Isar Aerospace.

Two valuation methods were applicable. The peer-median approach anchors to four same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.6B and a range of $1.0B to $2.5B. The stage-progression method applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier to the current $1.1B post-money, implying $2.0B at the next financing stage. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales could not be applied because Skyryse's revenue is not on file. The public sector median P/S of 64.2x across 6 tickers would require disclosed revenue to be meaningful.

Triangulating the two applicable methods yields a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.8B. The current valuation of $1.15B sits below this range, suggesting upside potential if capital deployment and revenue growth support the peer-set and stage-based multiples. Key uncertainties include near-term revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peer benchmarks, and sector-wide market conditions affecting late-stage funding multiples.
